post_rendered:
Mafia Game 275
Fire vs Water Mafia

  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Day chat will be enabled via an AoPS private forum.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • When the game starts respond to your role pm with a message in the town flavor color (#f60).
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • This game will partially use the voting rules philosophy of "if it looks like a vote, it's a vote". So spelling/capitalization does not matter so long as the “Vote:” part is right, and the following two rules are met.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>", or c or code text: 
      Vote: <insert player here>
      .
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang" or: 
      Vote: No Hang
      .
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though you have the ability to do so. Unvote in the format "Vote: Unvote" or: 
      Vote: Unvote
      . An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is hanged. If there is a tie the player who received the votes first is hanged. If No Hang is part of the tie, No Hang occurs.
    • Starting day 1, If more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • Starting day 1, If at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, it will be twilight and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I (or the comod) post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, you will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, they must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod or co-mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • I (NDMath) am the moderator of Mafia Game 275. Cosmosiq is the comod of Mafia Game 275.
    • This brown (#974E03) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar. This blue (color=blue) is Cosmosiq's mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The co-mod the second to last say.
    • Mafia fake claims will be treated symmetrically to town roles.

  • Read all of the rules
Story
A boat was approaching the eastside of the island. And this was a good thing since this was in fact the island the passengers were trying to get to. But this wasn't a perfect thing, for the passengers wanted to arrive on the westside, as that was the inhabited part, and traveling around the island was going to leave them quite vulnerable.

The Water God patrolled the eastern coast of the island. They were rather mysterious and liked the isolation. The Water God is patient and could destroy anything, given enough time. This particular boat could easily be defeated with a single whirlpool, but it would be far more joyful to the Water God to sink the boat at what would like the last possible moment for things to go wrong to the passengers. After all, it's not every day that a boat comes passing through these waters.

The Fire God recently took home to the eastern side of the island because of the dense forest. The Fire God was strong and active, often simply destroying whatever was in its path. Often viewed as careless but never afraid to stand up to the darkness of evil. Seeing what was at stake here, the Fire God ordered their minions to attack immediately. It may not be the wisest idea to attack water with fire, but the Fire God would not die easily either in this woodland.

There were rumors among the minions that Goddess Snow would see the action going on and come interfere. What her motives were, however, was unclear.


Setup
The setup is closed, but there is exactly 3 mafia and at most 1 third party aligned player.
Town is flavored as fire-based/aligned and Mafia is flavored as water-based/aligned.
Mafia are multitasking by default.

The action resolution for this game is Reasonable Action Resolution.
Private Chats are via AoPS forum with daychat enabled.
No action may self-target unless stated otherwise in the player's role pm.
Example Vanilla Townie Role PM wrote:
Welcome Cosmosiq to Mafia Game 275

You are a Fire Minion.

As a minion, you have no special abilities, but that could change!

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.
Example Mafia Goon Role PM wrote:
Welcome NDMath to Mafia Game 275

You are a Water Minion. The town role with matching abilities is called Fire Minion, however this does not confirm the existence of that role only the possibility.

As a minion, you have no special abilities, but that could change!
You may also submit your team's factional kill each night, and have access to a private factional chat.

You win when all fire-aligned players are dead.


Alive (1)
8. DXtreme - Water God

Dead (12)
10. dragonlouis - Water Minion
13. adrastea violetmx12 - Fire Minion
7. MinecraftArchitect lovematch13 - Fire Minion
9. candythecorgi ostriches88 - Fire Minion
12. saturnrocket - Water Minion
2. scannose - Heat
5. Aminecraftbear - Fire Minion
6. ibmo0907 aayr - Fire Minion
11. fdas - Fire Minion
1. WisteriaV - Candle
3. BananaBall00 = Fire God
4. cherryolive - Heat


Notable Stuff
Day 1 begins
adrastea replaces violetmx12
Note/Deadline Extension
MinecraftArchitect replaces lovematch13
End of Day 1; Start of Night 1
End of Night 1; Start of Day 2
candythecorgi replaces ostriches88
End of Day 2; Start of Night 2
ibmo0907 replaces aayr
End of Night 2; Start of Day 3
End of Day 3; Start of Night 3
End of Night 3; Start of Day 4
End of Game!


Do not post until this line is crossed out.
Current state: Game Over, Everyone may post

post_rendered:
The enthusiastic captain started sailing the ship around the island. The passengers remained unamused, but the captain did not pick up on this. Nor did he pick up on the fight between titans happening right around the boat. But no worry, if the Fire God's minions just kept everything under control, there would be no need for the oblivious boat captain to know.

It is now Day 1, you may post. Deadline is October 19th at 4:30 pm CDT. Majority and Deadline Hang are both enabled.

Additionally this is a reminder of the rule to not quote or pretend to quote pms from the mod, including role pms. This includes copypasting a chunk of such text into the thread even if not put in quotes. Paraphrasing is fine.

adrastea wrote:
For the record, I have not read past post 160. I am on the car right now, and will be unavailable for the next two or so hours, perhaps even longer, so I will not be able to fully catch up for quite a while. I have made it clear multiple times that I have not read the entire thread. I am allowed to vote who I wish, and the most appropriate slot for me to push is Banana at the moment. I have the most knowledge (or rather, least knowledge, since they are in fact a lowposter) on them at the moment, and they are the one player I find myself scumreading the most.

Out of curiosity, what is LHF?

LHF is low hanging fruit, which means targeting players who exhibit scumminess without a second thought. In general, going for LHF is a very anti town move because there is almost no motivation for town to push a quick hang on a player who is suspected to be scum as town, while there are plenty of benefits on the maf side such as minimizing interaction and less space to slip. Also is a form of fake contribution. If you want to see a prime example of how LHF is used, look at game 222. I basically almost won by persuading town to hang people who didn't know how to play and those who were inactive

dragonlouis wrote:
This is totally random.

Why not add a huge number of roles?
Oh, and by huge, I mean... huge...

Town
Town (Protective):

Doctor: Choose someone to protect from death each night, granting them Powerful Defense. Can only protect themselves once. (Roleblock Immune) (Control Immune While Self-Healing)

Bodyguard: Choose someone to guard each night, granting them Powerful Defense for that night. If the person you guard is attacked, you will counterattack the attacker and die. The Bodyguard may guard themselves while guarding another player if they choose. (Vest Charges: 1) (Attack: Powerful) (Defense: None/Basic)

Martial Artist: Choose someone to train in martial arts each night. That person will be granted Powerful Defense that night and will counterattack their assailant. The Martial Artist may not train someone 2 nights in a row. The Martial Artist may go on alert instead of training, similarly to the Veteran, if they choose. (Alert Charges: 1) (Attack: Powerful) (Defense: None/Basic) (Detection Vulnerable) (Control Immune While on Alert) (Roleblock Immune While on Alert)

Trapper: Every other night, may set up a trap at a player's house, granting them Powerful Defense. The Trapper can trap themselves even if they already have a trap up and vise-versa. Traps take one day to build and can be taken down if the Trapper chooses to do so. If a trapped player is the subject of a harmful visit, the trap will trigger, attacking the assailant. If a trapped player is the subject of a non-harmful visit, it will trigger, but the visitor will not be attacked. The Trapper will know the role of anyone who triggers a trap. (Attack: Powerful) (Roleblock Immune) (Control Immune)

Banshee: Each night, may select a target, granting them Basic Defense. If your target was attacked, you will be told the names of all visitors. Can choose to self-protect once, granting themselves Basic Defense. (Control Immune While Self-Protecting)

Town (Investigative):

Sheriff: Interrogate someone each night for suspicious behavior. Detection Immune and Detection Omniscient roles will appear innocent.

Judge: Investigate someone each night to see if they are a Lawful citizen. Detection Immune roles will appear as Unlawful to the Judge, but Detection Vulnerable roles will also appear as Unlawful.

Tracker: Can choose 1 person to see who they use their night action on.

Lookout: Check to see who visits someone each night.

Matchmaker: Choose 2 targets and see if their win conditions are contradictory. Detection Omni roles will appear as compatible with town members. The Jailor will appear incompatible with everyone.

Detective: Each night, may choose a target and learns if their target is a killing role.

Psychic: On odd-numbered Nights, the Psychic will receive a vision of three names, at least one of which is evil. On even-numbered Nights, the Psychic will receive a vision of two names, at least one of which is good.

Town (Aversive):

X-Man: Roleblock someone each night, will know the visitor if someone attempts to roleblock them. (Roleblock Immune)

Inquisitor: Roleblock someone each night, and find out who they were planning to visit. (Detection Vulnerable)

Martyr: Upon being killed, reveals the identity of their killer, has 3 heal charges from beyond the grave.

Transporter: Switch the fates of two people each night. (Control Immune)

Escape Artist: Each night, may choose to escape your house and roleblock everyone who uses their night action on you. You will be informed of the people you roleblock. If you are jailed by the Jailor, you will not be roleblocked, you cannot be executed, and the Jailor will be informed that you escaped from jail once the night ends. (Roleblock Immune) (Escape Charges: 4)

Town (Vengeance):

Retributionist: Can steal the ability of a dead town member, and use it. Can only use an ability the number of times that certain role could use when they were alive. (Charges: 4) (Control Immune) (Detection Vulnerable) (Unique)

Grave Robber: If a Town member is voted out, the Grave Robber gains 3 charges. They can then use these charges on healing, investigating, roleblocking, or killing somebody. If the Grave Robber kills a Town member, they can no longer use their charges to kill anyone. (Attack: Basic) (Charges per Town Member voted out: 3) (Detection Vulnerable) (Unique)

Sniper: Each night, target one player. If that player is attacked, attack the assailant. Cannot attack town members. (Attack: Unstoppable) (Detection Vulnerable)

Reviver: May resurrect 1 Town role per game. That person's role will be displayed. If the Reviver resurrects a non-Town role that is incorrectly displayed as a Town role in the Graveyard, the non-Town role will be revived and the Reviver will not be able to resurrect anyone else. (Unique)

Coroner: Each night, may check a dead body and find out who the dead player visited on the night of their death. By doing this, you will also learn the role of that player if their role was erased or is displayed incorrectly, and you will receive any GUI messages that player received on the night of their death. (Detection Vulnerable) (Control Immune)

Town (Elective):

Mayor: The Mayor gets 3 votes instead of 1, but only after they reveal themself. (Unique)

King: May grant someone the ability to have 2 votes during the next day, excluding themselves. The Town will be aware of this person. (Unique)



Town (Killing):

Jailor: Each night, may choose to haul someone off to jail. When jailed, a player is roleblocked, bypassing Roleblock Immunity, and no night actions work on the jailed player (Except for Unstoppable attacks). The Jailor may speak with their jailee at night. The Jailor may also execute their jailee if they choose, except on Night 1. If the Jailor executes a Town member, they lose the ability to jail. (Execute Charges: 2) (Detection Vulnerable) (Attack: Powerful) (Unique)

Veteran: Each night, may choose to go on alert at night. While alerting, the Veteran gains Basic Defense and attacks everyone who visits them. (Charges: 3) (Attack: Powerful) (Roleblock Immune) (Control Immune) (Unique)

Vigilante: May choose to kill someone each night, except Night 1. If the Vigilante kills a townsperson, they will die the next night. (Charges: 3) (Attack: Basic) (Detection Vulnerable)

Rancher: At night, may choose a target. If the target is non-town, roleblock and attack them. If the target is a Town member, the Rancher will not attack the target, only roleblock them, and the Rancher loses all of their charges afterward. (Charges: 2) (Attack: Powerful) (Roleblock Immune) (Unique)

Vampire Hunter: At night, may choose someone to stake. Staking someone will attack them if they are a Vampire. If the Vampire Hunter is visited by a Vampire, the Vampire will not be attacked, but the Vampire Hunter will not be converted. The Vampire Hunter will die if they are attacked by a Vampire. The Vampire Hunter can hear the whispers of the Vampire GC. After the Vampires are eliminated, the Vampire Hunter becomes a Vigilante with 1 charge. (Attack: Basic) (Unique)

Amplifier: Each night, may choose to attack a suspected evil. If the Amplifier votes against a non-town role that is then voted out on the same day, the Amplifier will gain a charge. If the Amplifier votes against a town role that is then voted out on the same day, they will lose all charges and lose the ability to gain charges. If the Amplifier accidentally kills a town member, they will lose all charges and lose the ability to gain charges. With each kill on an evil, the Amplifier upgrades their role.

Kill 1: Amplifier is now Roleblock Immune.

Kill 2: Amplifier now has a Powerful Attack.

Kill 3: Amplifier now roleblocks their target along with killing them.

Kill 4: Amplifier now has permanent Basic Defense.

Kill 5: Amplifier now has an Unstoppable Attack.

(Detection Vulnerable) (Charges: 1/Infinite) (Unique)


Mafia
Mafia (Killing):

Godfather: Kill someone each night. If there is a Mafioso, they will attack your target instead of you. If the Godfather dies, and there is no Mafioso, another mafia member will become the Mafioso depending upon their Priority. (Attack: Basic) (Defense: Basic) (Detection Omniscient) (Unique)

Mafioso: Attack the Godfather's target each night. If there is no Godfather, or if the Godfather does not give orders, the Mafioso will attack their target. If the Godfather dies, you will become the next Godfather. If you die, and there is no Godfather, another mafia member will become the new Mafioso depending upon their Priority. (Attack: Basic) (Control Immune with Godfather) (Unique)

Ambusher: Can prepare an ambush outside someone's house at night, killing 1 person who visits their target. All people who visited that target other than the person who died learns the Ambusher's identity. (Attack: Basic) (Unique) (Priority: 12)



Mafia (Deception):

Framer: Each night, may frame a town member. Any framed town members will show as suspicious to the Sheriff and Judge, incompatible with other townies to the Matchmaker, Killing to the Detective, and shows up as evil in the Psychic's vision. A Rancher will kill a townie if they have been framed. The Framer will passively frame anyone who visits them. (Priority: 11)

Stone Mask: Disguise a mafia member as a town member for the night and the following day. All investigative abilities used on this mafia member will reflect the selected townsperson instead. Upon the death of the disguised mafia member, the role of the selected townsperson is displayed. (Priority: 10)

Forger: Can choose to forge a player, replacing their role shown in the graveyard with any role in the game when they die. (Charges: 2) (Priority: 1)

Hypnotist: Each night, May hypnotize one player, giving them a false GUI message of the Hypnotist’s choice once the night ends. (Detection Immune) (Priority: 7)

Janitor: Each night, may target a player and erase their role. (Charges: 4) (Priority: 4)



Mafia (Coercion):

Hacker: Can choose 1 person to Hack. Hacked players must vote for a person of the Hacker's choosing during the following day. Hackers can Hack their mafia members if they choose, but Hacking the Mayor will be considered a harmful visit. If the Mayor is targeted, they will only be unable to vote. (Detection Immune) (Priority: 6)

Puppet Master: Can control someone's actions, and gains knowledge of their identity. (Charges: 4) (Control Immune) (Priority: 5)

Briber: During Day 4 or later, may choose to ask a player if they would like to join the side of the Mafia at night. If the player accepts, they keep their role, but their alignment switches to the Mafia. If they decline, they receive a vision with 2 players, one of which will be the Briber. Attempting to bribe someone roleblocks them. The Briber may attempt to bribe more than 1 person if the first person declined. Everyone who the Briber bribes will be Framed. (Detection Immune) (Priority: 12)

Consigliere: Investigate someone each night to learn their exact role. (Priority: 9)

Consort: Roleblock someone each night. (Roleblock Immune) (Priority: 8)

Junkie: Each night, the Junkie may make a certain mafia member's role stronger for 1 night. If the Junkie chooses, they can Rampage at someone's house instead of making a mafia member stronger. Although, if they choose to do this, they may not use their night action for 1 night afterward.

Assassin: Can make it to where the Godfather/Mafioso's attack becomes an Powerful Attack, and the Godfather/Mafioso's visit becomes Astral. However, the Godfather/Mafioso will not be saved from attacks they may be a victim of. (Charges: 2) (Detection Immune) (Priority: 2)

ㅤ

Buffs:

Godfather/Mafioso: The Godfather now Rampages at someone's house that night.

Framer: The Framer can "un-frame" a member of the mafia once per game, making them detection omniscient.

Ambusher: The Ambusher's identity is not revealed even if someone sees them prepare an ambush that night.

Stone Mask: The Stone Mask is now Detection Omniscient.

Forger: The Forger now also forges how their target was killed that night.

Assassin: The Godfather is now granted Powerful Defense that night.

Hypnotist: The Hypnotist can give a false GUI message to two people that night.

Janitor: The Janitor gains 1 extra charge.

Hacker: The Hacker can now force the Mayor to vote someone of their choosing. This will still be a harmful visit.

Puppet Master: The Puppet Master learns the role of the person they controlled their target into that night.

Briber: If a player rejects the bribe, they no longer receive a vision.

Consigliere: The Consigliere's ability is now no longer affected by douses or hexes.

Consort: The Consort now bypasses Roleblock Immunity.

(Buff Charges: 4) (Rampage Charges: 1) (Priority: 3)

other parties:

Coven
Coven (Killing):

Coven Leader: Each night, may control someone's actions, and gains knowledge of their identity. Their action is considered harmful, even when not directly attacking. (Control Immune) (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 1) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Coven Leader's first target is attacked, and the Coven Leader gains Basic Defense.

Poisoner: Each night, may poison someone. Poison will kill a target the night after it is administered, and can be cured by a Doctor. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 3) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Doctor can no longer heal Poison, and the Poisoner's visits are now Astral.

Hex Master: Each night, may hex someone. When all non-Coven players are hexed, all non-Coven players die. This ability does not take effect if the Hex Master is dead. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 2) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Hex Masters, if they so choose, can attack the player they hex.

Rune Master: Every night, may place a runes at a player's home or activate a placed rune. When a placed rune is activated, it will kill the player on who the Rune Master placed it. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 6) (Astral Attack) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Rune Master can now place up to 3 runes or activate up to 3 runes.



Coven (Coercion):

Necromancer: Each night, the Necromancer may use the ability of a dead player. The Necromancer cannot use a dead player's ability more than once. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 4) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Necromancer may summon a ghoul to attack their target by selecting themselves as their first target.

Potion Master: Each night, may choose to use a healing potion, a revealing potion, or an attacking potion. Each potion has a 3-Day cooldown. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 5) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Potion Master's potions no longer have a cooldown.

Medusa: Each night, may choose to Stone Gaze, killing anyone who visits Medusa. This also erases their role. (Charges: 3) (Roleblock Immune While Gazing) (Attack: Powerful) (Priority: 7) (Unique)

Necronomicon: Medusa can now visit players to turn them to stone.


Mutants
Mutants:

Mutants normally appear as Guilty to the Sheriff and Judge.



Mutant (Killing):

Alpha Mutant/Delta Mutant: Each night, may attack someone. The Alpha Mutant may also choose to emit toxic sludge from their home, granting them Basic Defense, and Basic Attacking any visitors. If the Alpha Mutant dies, the Mutant with the highest Priority will be promoted to a Delta Mutant. Delta Mutants only have a Basic Attack. The sludge charges that each Alpha/Delta Mutant uses affect how many a promoted Delta Mutant would have. A Delta Mutant's sludge will not grant the Delta Mutant Basic Defense. (Attack: Powerful/Basic) (Defense: None/Basic) (Sludge Charges: 3) (Unique)

Avenger: Upon the death of a Mutant, may rampage at someone's home. If the Avenger is promoted to Delta Mutant, they keep their original ability and may rampage in addition to their original attack and sludge charge. Due to this, a promoted Avenger will be named "Delta Avenger." (Priority: 1) (Attack: Powerful) (Unique)

Sludger: Can sludge outside a Mutant's house at night, killing 1 person who visits their target. All people who visited that target other than the person who died learn the Sludger's identity. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 5)



Mutant (Investigative):

Owl: Each night, may choose to watch the homes of the mutants. This will inform the Owl of who visited each mutant member. (Priority: 3)



Mutant (Coercion):

Morph: Each night, may choose to reveal a player’s identity. When at least 1 identity has been revealed, that role goes into the Morph’s library. During any night, the Morph may choose to use one of the roles in their library instead of revealing a role’s identity. When the Morph does this, they can no longer reveal identities, but they gain 1 charge of each role’s ability in their library. (Priority: 7) (Unique)

Agitator: Each night, may choose to taunt the town, making everyone who was going to visit a Mutant visit the Agitator instead. The Agitator will gain Powerful Defense while taunting. (Priority: 2) (Taunt Charges: 3) (Detection Omniscient) (Roleblock Immune) (Unique)

Fogger: Each night, may choose to shroud someone's house in fog. Shrouded players will roleblock everyone who visits them. (Charges: 4) (Priority: 4)

Bomber: Every night, may choose to plant a bomb in a player’s brain. This will allow the Bomber to see who they visit at night on the night the bomb was placed. Bombed players who visit Mutants can be attacked by the Bomber on any of the following nights. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 6) (Unique)


Neutrals
Neutral (Chaotic):

Pirate: Can choose during the day to duel someone every night. When in a duel, the Pirate chooses a method of attack, and their target chooses a method of defense. Each method of attack corresponds with one method of defense. If the Pirate and their target choose the corresponding attack/defense method, then the Pirate kills their target. If they do not, then the Pirate does not kill their target. Dueling someone roleblocks them. The Pirate must win 2 duels to win the game. The Pirate may not duel the same person twice in a row.

Scimitar -> Sidestep

Rapier -> Chainmail

Pistol -> Backpedal

(Attack: Powerful) (Roleblock Immune) (Control Immune) (Unique)

Vampire: Each night, may choose a target. On odd nights, converts the target to vampirism. On even nights, kills the target. A role with Defense will not be converted. If a Mafia/Coven/Mutant member is converted, they will die instead. The youngest Vampire will visit the target. The first Vampire to spawn in will be granted Basic Defense, and will be named "Vampire Master". (Attack: Basic) (Detection Vulnerable)

Drifter: Each Full Moon Night, may kill someone. Must kill 2 people to win. Will lose all charges if they win. (Charges: 3) (Detection Vulnerable) (Attack: Basic) (Unique)



.

Neutral (Benign):

Survivor: Must survive to the end of the match. Can grant itself Basic Defense. (Charges: 4)

Bounty Hunter: Can choose to kill someone each night. In order to win, Bounty Hunter must kill a certain role during the night. If the Bounty Hunter kills the wrong person, or if their target dies by other means, they become a Survivor with no vests. (Attack: Powerful) (Defense: Basic) (Charges: 1)

Amnesiac: Can become a role in the graveyard. Must fulfill that role's win condition to win. If the Amnesiac dies on night 1, they do not win, but they may attack their killer. (Attack: Unstoppable)

Guardian Angel: Each night, may choose to protect their target. Protecting their target grants them Powerful Defense, and they cannot be voted against the next day. To win, the Guardian Angel's target must survive until the end of the game. If the Guardian Angel's target dies, they become a Survivor with no vests. (Charges: 2)

Wanderer: When attacked for the first time, you join the faction of your attacker. If attacked multiple times in one night, join the faction that decided on their night action earlier. Loses Invincible Defense after being converted. If attacked by a Neutral role, gains the abilities and win condition of that role. The Wanderer will be informed of those who attack them.

(Defense: Invincible/None)

.

Neutral (Evil):

Witch: Each night, may control someone, and learns their identity. Their action is harmful. Can win with any evil faction. Cannot co-exist with a CL. (Detection Omniscient) (Defense: Basic) (Unique)

Jester: Must be voted out by the Town during the day. If the Jester is voted out, they may choose one of their guilty or abstaining voters to kill the following night. If the Jester dies on night 1, they do not win, but they may attack their killer. (Attack: Unstoppable)

Executioner: Has a target that they must trick the Town into voting out during the day. The Executioner's target will always be a Town member, but never the Mayor. If the Executioner's target dies during the night, the Executioner becomes a Jester. (Defense: Basic)

Ape: Must die at night to win. If the Ape is killed at night, they may then kill one of the players that voted against them during the day or an abstainer. If no players voted against the Ape, the Ape does not win even if they die at night. If the Ape dies on Night 1 however, they do win but cannot maul their attacker. (Attack: Unstoppable) (Framed)

.

Neutral (Killing):

Neutral Killing roles normally appear as Guilty to the Sheriff and Judge.



Serial Killer: Each night, may kill someone. If the Serial Killer is roleblocked, the roleblocker will receive a basic attack along with the original target. This also applies with the jailor. The SK will not attack the jailor if they are executed. To win, the Serial Killer must be the last player standing. (Attack: Basic) (Defense: Basic) (Roleblock Immune)

Arsonist: Each night, may douse someone in gasoline or ignite doused targets. Players who are doused will be framed, and everyone who visits the Arsonist will be passively doused. The Arsonist may wait a day to clean themselves of gasoline if they are doused by a fellow Arsonist, or douse themselves. (Detection Omniscient) (Attack: Unstoppable) (Defense: Basic)

Werewolf: Each Full Moon Night, may rampage at someone's home. If the Werewolf stays home, they will rampage at home. The Werewolf is Detection Omniscient on Non-Full Moon Nights. (Attack: Powerful) (Defense: Basic)

Marauder: Each night, may mark someone. A marked player will die if anyone else visits them. If they so choose, the Marauder can target an already marked player and kill them, Powerful Attacking them. Anyone who visits the Marauder will be marked. To win, you must be the last person standing. Marking someone unmarked will not be considered an attack. However, marking an already marked player will be considered an attack. If another player visits a marked player, triggering the mark, it will be considered an attack, and any protective roles that are kill capable will attack that player instead of the Marauder. To win, the Marauder must be the last player standing. (Attack: Basic/Powerful) (Defense: Basic) (Detection Immune) (Control Immune) (Unique)

Vulture: Each night, may kill someone. If the Vulture chooses to, they can buff their attack from Basic to Powerful for a night, and erase their target's role along with killing them. This can only be done on a Full Moon Night. Erased roles will show up as "Decomposed" in the graveyard, but the Vulture will secretly know their target's role. To win, the Vulture must be the last player standing. (Decompose Charges: 2) (Attack: Basic) (Defense: Basic) (Detection Immune)

Juggernaut: Each night, may kill someone. With every kill, the Juggernaut gets stronger. To win, the Juggernaut must be the last person standing.

Kill 1: The Juggernaut is now Detection Omniscient, Control Immune, and Roleblock Immune.

Kill 2: The Juggernaut now has a Powerful Attack.

Kill 3: The Juggernaut now Rampages at their target's house and Rampages at home if they do not attack.

Kill 4: The Juggernaut now Rampages at home even if they do attack.

Kill 5: The Juggernaut now has an Unstoppable Attack.

(Attack: Basic/Powerful/Unstoppable) (Defense: Basic) (Unique)

.

Neutral (Confirm-Specific):

Confirm-Specific roles cannot appear unless there is a confirmed slot for them.

Idiot: The Idiot will not know that they rolled Idiot, and the moderator will tell them that they rolled a random Town Investigative role. Each night, the Idiot will receive incorrect results about their target. To win, the Idiot must survive until the end of the game or get voted out.


Terms used above
Attack/Defense Values:

Attack:

None - Your role cannot attack players directly.

Basic - Your attacks will kill any role with a Defense value of None.

Powerful - Your attacks will kill any role with a Defense value of Basic.

Unstoppable - Your attacks will kill any role with a Defense value of Powerful.

Defense:

None - Your role will die to any Attack.

Basic - Your role is protected from Basic Attacks.

Powerful - Your role is protected from Powerful Attacks.

Invincible - Your role is protected from Unstoppable Attacks.



Terms:

Priority: The order in which certain Mafia/Mutant roles are promoted to Godfather/Alpha Mutant, or in which certain Coven members get the Necronomicon (btw coven isn't added here yet but it will be eventually!)

Charges: The amount of times a role can use its night action.

Unique: Unique roles can only appear in the game once.

Rampage: Attack your target and everyone who visits your target.

Detection Vulnerable: Detection Vulnerable roles appear as innocent to the Sheriff, but guilty to the Judge.

Detection Immune: Detection Immune roles appear as innocent to the Sheriff, but guilty to the Judge.

Detection Omniscient: Detection Omniscient roles cannot be found as suspicious.

Roleblock Immune: Roleblock immune roles cannot be roleblocked.

Control Immune: Control Immune roles cannot be controlled.

Framed: A Framed player shows up as suspicious to the Sheriff and Judge.

Full Moon Night: Nights 2, 4, and every night after Night 4.

Necronomicon: A power-up that a Coven member gets. A Coven member gets the Necronomicon on Night 3.

Astral Visit: A night action where the targeted player isn't considered visited.

[quote=dragonlouis]This is totally random.

Why not add a huge number of roles?
Oh, and by huge, I mean... huge...

[hide=Town]Town (Protective):

Doctor: Choose someone to protect from death each night, granting them Powerful Defense. Can only protect themselves once. (Roleblock Immune) (Control Immune While Self-Healing)

Bodyguard: Choose someone to guard each night, granting them Powerful Defense for that night. If the person you guard is attacked, you will counterattack the attacker and die. The Bodyguard may guard themselves while guarding another player if they choose. (Vest Charges: 1) (Attack: Powerful) (Defense: None/Basic)

Martial Artist: Choose someone to train in martial arts each night. That person will be granted Powerful Defense that night and will counterattack their assailant. The Martial Artist may not train someone 2 nights in a row. The Martial Artist may go on alert instead of training, similarly to the Veteran, if they choose. (Alert Charges: 1) (Attack: Powerful) (Defense: None/Basic) (Detection Vulnerable) (Control Immune While on Alert) (Roleblock Immune While on Alert)

Trapper: Every other night, may set up a trap at a player's house, granting them Powerful Defense. The Trapper can trap themselves even if they already have a trap up and vise-versa. Traps take one day to build and can be taken down if the Trapper chooses to do so. If a trapped player is the subject of a harmful visit, the trap will trigger, attacking the assailant. If a trapped player is the subject of a non-harmful visit, it will trigger, but the visitor will not be attacked. The Trapper will know the role of anyone who triggers a trap. (Attack: Powerful) (Roleblock Immune) (Control Immune)

Banshee: Each night, may select a target, granting them Basic Defense. If your target was attacked, you will be told the names of all visitors. Can choose to self-protect once, granting themselves Basic Defense. (Control Immune While Self-Protecting)

Town (Investigative):

Sheriff: Interrogate someone each night for suspicious behavior. Detection Immune and Detection Omniscient roles will appear innocent.

Judge: Investigate someone each night to see if they are a Lawful citizen. Detection Immune roles will appear as Unlawful to the Judge, but Detection Vulnerable roles will also appear as Unlawful.

Tracker: Can choose 1 person to see who they use their night action on.

Lookout: Check to see who visits someone each night.

Matchmaker: Choose 2 targets and see if their win conditions are contradictory. Detection Omni roles will appear as compatible with town members. The Jailor will appear incompatible with everyone.

Detective: Each night, may choose a target and learns if their target is a killing role.

Psychic: On odd-numbered Nights, the Psychic will receive a vision of three names, at least one of which is evil. On even-numbered Nights, the Psychic will receive a vision of two names, at least one of which is good.

Town (Aversive): 

X-Man: Roleblock someone each night, will know the visitor if someone attempts to roleblock them. (Roleblock Immune)

Inquisitor: Roleblock someone each night, and find out who they were planning to visit. (Detection Vulnerable)

Martyr: Upon being killed, reveals the identity of their killer, has 3 heal charges from beyond the grave.

Transporter: Switch the fates of two people each night. (Control Immune)

Escape Artist: Each night, may choose to escape your house and roleblock everyone who uses their night action on you. You will be informed of the people you roleblock. If you are jailed by the Jailor, you will not be roleblocked, you cannot be executed, and the Jailor will be informed that you escaped from jail once the night ends. (Roleblock Immune) (Escape Charges: 4)

Town (Vengeance): 

Retributionist: Can steal the ability of a dead town member, and use it. Can only use an ability the number of times that certain role could use when they were alive. (Charges: 4) (Control Immune) (Detection Vulnerable) (Unique)

Grave Robber: If a Town member is voted out, the Grave Robber gains 3 charges. They can then use these charges on healing, investigating, roleblocking, or killing somebody. If the Grave Robber kills a Town member, they can no longer use their charges to kill anyone. (Attack: Basic) (Charges per Town Member voted out: 3) (Detection Vulnerable) (Unique)

Sniper: Each night, target one player. If that player is attacked, attack the assailant. Cannot attack town members. (Attack: Unstoppable) (Detection Vulnerable)

Reviver: May resurrect 1 Town role per game. That person's role will be displayed. If the Reviver resurrects a non-Town role that is incorrectly displayed as a Town role in the Graveyard, the non-Town role will be revived and the Reviver will not be able to resurrect anyone else. (Unique)

Coroner: Each night, may check a dead body and find out who the dead player visited on the night of their death. By doing this, you will also learn the role of that player if their role was erased or is displayed incorrectly, and you will receive any GUI messages that player received on the night of their death. (Detection Vulnerable) (Control Immune)

Town (Elective):

Mayor: The Mayor gets 3 votes instead of 1, but only after they reveal themself. (Unique)

King: May grant someone the ability to have 2 votes during the next day, excluding themselves. The Town will be aware of this person. (Unique)

 

Town (Killing):

Jailor: Each night, may choose to haul someone off to jail. When jailed, a player is roleblocked, bypassing Roleblock Immunity, and no night actions work on the jailed player (Except for Unstoppable attacks). The Jailor may speak with their jailee at night. The Jailor may also execute their jailee if they choose, except on Night 1. If the Jailor executes a Town member, they lose the ability to jail. (Execute Charges: 2) (Detection Vulnerable) (Attack: Powerful) (Unique)

Veteran: Each night, may choose to go on alert at night. While alerting, the Veteran gains Basic Defense and attacks everyone who visits them. (Charges: 3) (Attack: Powerful) (Roleblock Immune) (Control Immune) (Unique)

Vigilante: May choose to kill someone each night, except Night 1. If the Vigilante kills a townsperson, they will die the next night. (Charges: 3) (Attack: Basic) (Detection Vulnerable)

Rancher: At night, may choose a target. If the target is non-town, roleblock and attack them. If the target is a Town member, the Rancher will not attack the target, only roleblock them, and the Rancher loses all of their charges afterward. (Charges: 2) (Attack: Powerful) (Roleblock Immune) (Unique) 

Vampire Hunter: At night, may choose someone to stake. Staking someone will attack them if they are a Vampire. If the Vampire Hunter is visited by a Vampire, the Vampire will not be attacked, but the Vampire Hunter will not be converted. The Vampire Hunter will die if they are attacked by a Vampire. The Vampire Hunter can hear the whispers of the Vampire GC. After the Vampires are eliminated, the Vampire Hunter becomes a Vigilante with 1 charge. (Attack: Basic) (Unique)

Amplifier: Each night, may choose to attack a suspected evil. If the Amplifier votes against a non-town role that is then voted out on the same day, the Amplifier will gain a charge. If the Amplifier votes against a town role that is then voted out on the same day, they will lose all charges and lose the ability to gain charges. If the Amplifier accidentally kills a town member, they will lose all charges and lose the ability to gain charges. With each kill on an evil, the Amplifier upgrades their role.

Kill 1: Amplifier is now Roleblock Immune.

Kill 2: Amplifier now has a Powerful Attack.

Kill 3: Amplifier now roleblocks their target along with killing them.

Kill 4: Amplifier now has permanent Basic Defense.

Kill 5: Amplifier now has an Unstoppable Attack.

(Detection Vulnerable) (Charges: 1/Infinite) (Unique)[/hide]

[hide=Mafia]Mafia (Killing):

Godfather: Kill someone each night. If there is a Mafioso, they will attack your target instead of you. If the Godfather dies, and there is no Mafioso, another mafia member will become the Mafioso depending upon their Priority. (Attack: Basic) (Defense: Basic) (Detection Omniscient) (Unique)

Mafioso: Attack the Godfather's target each night. If there is no Godfather, or if the Godfather does not give orders, the Mafioso will attack their target. If the Godfather dies, you will become the next Godfather. If you die, and there is no Godfather, another mafia member will become the new Mafioso depending upon their Priority. (Attack: Basic) (Control Immune with Godfather) (Unique)

Ambusher: Can prepare an ambush outside someone's house at night, killing 1 person who visits their target. All people who visited that target other than the person who died learns the Ambusher's identity. (Attack: Basic) (Unique) (Priority: 12)

 

Mafia (Deception):

Framer: Each night, may frame a town member. Any framed town members will show as suspicious to the Sheriff and Judge, incompatible with other townies to the Matchmaker, Killing to the Detective, and shows up as evil in the Psychic's vision. A Rancher will kill a townie if they have been framed. The Framer will passively frame anyone who visits them. (Priority: 11)

Stone Mask: Disguise a mafia member as a town member for the night and the following day. All investigative abilities used on this mafia member will reflect the selected townsperson instead. Upon the death of the disguised mafia member, the role of the selected townsperson is displayed. (Priority: 10)

Forger: Can choose to forge a player, replacing their role shown in the graveyard with any role in the game when they die. (Charges: 2) (Priority: 1)

Hypnotist: Each night, May hypnotize one player, giving them a false GUI message of the Hypnotist’s choice once the night ends. (Detection Immune) (Priority: 7)

Janitor: Each night, may target a player and erase their role. (Charges: 4) (Priority: 4)

 

Mafia (Coercion):

Hacker: Can choose 1 person to Hack. Hacked players must vote for a person of the Hacker's choosing during the following day. Hackers can Hack their mafia members if they choose, but Hacking the Mayor will be considered a harmful visit. If the Mayor is targeted, they will only be unable to vote. (Detection Immune) (Priority: 6)

Puppet Master: Can control someone's actions, and gains knowledge of their identity. (Charges: 4) (Control Immune) (Priority: 5)

Briber: During Day 4 or later, may choose to ask a player if they would like to join the side of the Mafia at night. If the player accepts, they keep their role, but their alignment switches to the Mafia. If they decline, they receive a vision with 2 players, one of which will be the Briber. Attempting to bribe someone roleblocks them. The Briber may attempt to bribe more than 1 person if the first person declined. Everyone who the Briber bribes will be Framed. (Detection Immune) (Priority: 12)

Consigliere: Investigate someone each night to learn their exact role. (Priority: 9)

Consort: Roleblock someone each night. (Roleblock Immune) (Priority: 8)

Junkie: Each night, the Junkie may make a certain mafia member's role stronger for 1 night. If the Junkie chooses, they can Rampage at someone's house instead of making a mafia member stronger. Although, if they choose to do this, they may not use their night action for 1 night afterward.

Assassin: Can make it to where the Godfather/Mafioso's attack becomes an Powerful Attack, and the Godfather/Mafioso's visit becomes Astral. However, the Godfather/Mafioso will not be saved from attacks they may be a victim of. (Charges: 2) (Detection Immune) (Priority: 2)

ㅤ

Buffs:

Godfather/Mafioso: The Godfather now Rampages at someone's house that night.

Framer: The Framer can "un-frame" a member of the mafia once per game, making them detection omniscient. 

Ambusher: The Ambusher's identity is not revealed even if someone sees them prepare an ambush that night.

Stone Mask: The Stone Mask is now Detection Omniscient.

Forger: The Forger now also forges how their target was killed that night.

Assassin: The Godfather is now granted Powerful Defense that night.

Hypnotist: The Hypnotist can give a false GUI message to two people that night.

Janitor: The Janitor gains 1 extra charge.

Hacker: The Hacker can now force the Mayor to vote someone of their choosing. This will still be a harmful visit. 

Puppet Master: The Puppet Master learns the role of the person they controlled their target into that night.

Briber: If a player rejects the bribe, they no longer receive a vision. 

Consigliere: The Consigliere's ability is now no longer affected by douses or hexes.

Consort: The Consort now bypasses Roleblock Immunity.

(Buff Charges: 4) (Rampage Charges: 1) (Priority: 3)[/hide]
other parties:

Poisoner: Each night, may poison someone. Poison will kill a target the night after it is administered, and can be cured by a Doctor. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 3) (Unique)

Hex Master: Each night, may hex someone. When all non-Coven players are hexed, all non-Coven players die. This ability does not take effect if the Hex Master is dead. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 2) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Hex Masters, if they so choose, can attack the player they hex.

Rune Master: Every night, may place a runes at a player's home or activate a placed rune. When a placed rune is activated, it will kill the player on who the Rune Master placed it. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 6) (Astral Attack) (Unique)

Necronomicon: The Necromancer may summon a ghoul to attack their target by selecting themselves as their first target.

Potion Master: Each night, may choose to use a healing potion, a revealing potion, or an attacking potion. Each potion has a 3-Day cooldown. (Attack: Basic) (Priority: 5) (Unique)

anyway, this is basically textbook scumhunting:
HannahW wrote:
Terms and things:
  • Things that scum tend to do:
    • bandwagoning (following the crowd)
    • lurking (not showing up)
    • pocketing (sweet talking a townie into locking them town)
    • hedging (having no definitive stance/fencesitting)
    • bussing (attacking their partner to seem not w/w, usually in the form of noncommittal attacks)
    • buddying (opposite of bussing for the purpose of making sure their partner is tr'ed, I think).
    • Making up random nonsense that derails things subtlely.
  • Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.
  • Terms to know:
    • w/w (wolf/wolf. In other words, two people are scum together.)
    • v/v (villager/villager. Two people are town together.)
    • w/v (self explanatory)
    • AI (alignment indicative)
    • NAI (not alignment indicative)
    • reads (thoughts on who is scum or town)
    • RVS (random voting stage: When people randomly vote to create something to discuss.)
    • meta (how one has played in previous mafia games)
    • wifom (wine in front of me).
    • Townread/lean (You think they're town or are leaning that way)
    • Scumread/lean (see above but for scum)

HannahW wrote:
Terms and things:
  • Things that scum tend to do:
    • bandwagoning (following the crowd)
    • lurking (not showing up)
    • pocketing (sweet talking a townie into locking them town)
    • hedging (having no definitive stance/fencesitting)
    • bussing (attacking their partner to seem not w/w, usually in the form of noncommittal attacks)
    • buddying (opposite of bussing for the purpose of making sure their partner is tr'ed, I think).
    • Making up random nonsense that derails things subtlely.
  • Motivation reads basically involves questioning yourself: is scum or town more likely to say/do this/that. Be aware of the assumptions you make when you make these kinds of reads.
  • Terms to know:
    • w/w (wolf/wolf. In other words, two people are scum together.)
    • v/v (villager/villager. Two people are town together.)
    • w/v (self explanatory)
    • AI (alignment indicative)
    • NAI (not alignment indicative)
    • reads (thoughts on who is scum or town)
    • RVS (random voting stage: When people randomly vote to create something to discuss.)
    • meta (how one has played in previous mafia games)
    • wifom (wine in front of me).
    • Townread/lean (You think they're town or are leaning that way)
    • Scumread/lean (see above but for scum)

post_rendered:
The reason I suspect the ostriches/candy slot is because of the lack of any effort made to save dragon on day 1. No real counterwagon was put up and dragon was simply abandoned to die. This makes me think that his team consisted of one active busser and one inactive member. And ostriches would be that inactive member since he wasn't around to do anything. Now I had low activity too but at least I was regularly checking into the thread and was aware of what was going on. I dropped half hearted statements saying that I didnt really think the arguments concerning dragon were compelling. That might seem scummy, but if I was actually serious about saving dragon, I would have started setting up a counterwagon because with no alternative dragon is still going to be the one dying. And if I was committed to distancing myself from dragon I wouldn't have put up a half hearted defense of him. If I was partners with dragon, doing what I did would be the worst of both worlds where I do nothing to actually prevent him from being hanged while making myself look suspicious.

Also, if dragon was on my team, he would not have made such an egregiously bad roleclaim. I would have taken a minute or two to right up a nice spiffy plausible fakeclaim either at the start of the game or when I saw he was under pressure. I might not have had the time to be constantly up to date on a fast moving thread, but I still go on this forum every day because of all the stuff I have to do and when I'm scum I have email notifications turned on for the private chat. I am not so checked out of the game that I would have let a claim as bad as that slide.

Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 275

You are Heat.

As heat, each night you get to target a player, and if they are a minion grant them an ability which they will possess starting the following day assuming they are still alive.
Night 1: Immunity to the first fire-based kill attempt on them
Night 2: No actions besides kills will have any effect on them, but they can not perform any actions either
Night 3: Vengeful, meaning if they die from the hang, they may attempt to take a player down with them
Night 4: Vigilante, meaning each night they may attempt to kill a player

This action is both mandatory and roaming. This means you can not target a player you targeted on a previous night, and if you fail to submit an action your action will, if possible, be randomized onto a random valid target.

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.

Candle Role PM wrote:
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 275

You are a Candle.

Each night you may target a player, if they are fire-aligned or are visited by a fire-aligned your candle will become lit. If they are water-aligned or visited by a water-aligned, your candle will be put out. Result from a visitor takes priority over the result from your target. If visited by multiple players, and any are water-aligned, your candle will be put out. Each morning you will be informed of the status of your candle. If your candle ends the night lit, you lose this ability.

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.


---
It is now Night 4.

The fighting would be over soon. Amazingly, the boat passengers hadn't yet pieced together what was going on. The boat captain had almost been flung overboard three times now, but remarkably was still sailing the ship the same as ever.
The Fire God and Water God finally faced off against one another, with almost all the minions cleared out, but of course, only one could come out on top, and Water triumphs against Fire.

BananaBall00 has died. BananaBall00 was the Fire God. Fire God role pm
Fire God role pm wrote:
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 275

You are the Fire God.

Rain of Fire - Each night you may target a player to attempt to kill them.
Spreading (passive) - Upon your death, your role will be inherented by a random living Fire Minion, if there are any.
Giant Flame (passive) - You will survive any fire-based kill attempts on you.

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.

cherryolive has died. cherryolive was Heat. Heat role pm
Heat role pm wrote:
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 275

You are Heat.

As heat, each night you get to target a player, and if they are a minion grant them an ability which they will possess starting the following day assuming they are still alive.
Night 1: Immunity to the first fire-based kill attempt on them
Night 2: No actions besides kills will have any effect on them, but they can not perform any actions either
Night 3: Vengeful, meaning if they die from the hang, they may attempt to take a player down with them
Night 4: Vigilante, meaning each night they may attempt to kill a player

This action is both mandatory and roaming. This means you can not target a player you targeted on a previous night, and if you fail to submit an action your action will, if possible, be randomized onto a random valid target.

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.


A tsunami wiped over the eastern side of the island, quickly extinguishing what remained of the fire, not that there was anything left. The boat also was hit by some ripples and started sinking. Fortunately, they were close to the shoreline and given the captain's questionable ability, the passengers had already put on life vests meaning there was hope for them. There was however no hope for the side of Fire as the Water God would live on.

DXtreme survived. DXtreme was the Water God. Water God Role pm
Water God Role PM wrote:
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 275

You are the Water God. There is no town role with exactly matching abilities.

Splash - Each night you may target a player to prevent other actions, besides kills, from succeeding on that player that night.
Flooding - Each night, starting night 2, you may target a player to attempt to roleblock them.
Persistence (passive) - After your death you will be treestumped. This means that you can no longer be targeted with or use actions, nor vote or be voted for, nor be subjected to activity checks, but you may still post including in private chats and your passive abilities will apply. Hindering other player's ability to read the game thread or play the game or similar will result in you losing the ability to post in thread after death.
Tsunami (passive) - When the living water-aligned make up at least 50% of all living players, all players will instantaneously be killed that are not water-aligned.
Mist (passive) - When a rival god is fully removed from the game, you will be notified.
Reflection (passive) - You will be informed of the role pm of (but not playername) any player that uses an action on you.
Rock Ice (passive) - You will survive any fire-based kill attempts on you.

You may also submit your team's factional kill each night, and have access to a private factional chat.

You win when all fire-aligned players are dead.


Congrats to the mafia team of DXtreme (Water God), saturnrocket (Water Minion), and dragonlouis (Water Minion)!
Everyone may post.

Heat Role PM wrote:
Welcome USERNAME to [url=]Mafia Game 27X[/url]

You are Heat.

As heat, each night you get to target a player, and if they are a minion grant them an ability which they will possess starting the following day assuming they are still alive.
Night 1: Immunity to the first fire-based kill attempt on them
Night 2: No actions besides kills will have any effect on them, but they can not perform any actions either
Night 3: Vengeful, meaning if they die from the hang, they may attempt to take a player down with them
Night 4: Vigilante, meaning each night they may attempt to kill a player

This action is both mandatory and roaming. This means you can not target a player you targeted on a previous night, and if you fail to submit an action your action will, if possible, be randomized onto a random valid target.

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.

Giant Flame (passive) - You will survive any fire-based kill attempts on you.

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.

1 Candle
Candle Role PM wrote:

Each night you may target a player, if they are fire-aligned or are visited by a fire-aligned your candle will become lit. If they are water-aligned or visited by a water-aligned, your candle will be put out. Result from a visitor takes priority over the result from your target. If visited by multiple players, and any are water-aligned, your candle will be put out. Each morning you will be informed of the status of your candle. If your candle ends the night lit, you lose this ability.

You win when all water-aligned players are dead.

I guess I think in the case of the Water God you can't really argue a particular ability was too op, just maybe that the set of abilities as a whole was a bit too strong. (I'm currently mixed on that, but one could make a valid argument.)

-it didn't have an effect on this game as it was never triggered, wasn't gonna be triggered, and I think DX forgot about it for a while
-it's not the Water God's strongest ability, flooding is definitely stronger, although that ability also only had an indirect effect on this game (made scumteam comfortable keeping wisteria alive), and maybe reflection is stronger too.
